#### diff --git a/scripts/auto_review_deps.py b/scripts/auto_review_deps.py  
#### new file mode 100755  
#### index 00000000..6ce6c0fa  
#### --- /dev/null  
#### +++ b/scripts/auto_review_deps.py  
#### @@ -0,0 +1,426 @@  
#### +#!/usr/bin/env python3  
#### +"""  
#### +Auto-review logic for dependency PRs in cryptotrader.  
#### +  
#### +Checks CI status and applies auto-approval or adds comments based on  
#### +the rules defined in the dependency-pr-automation skill.  
#### +  
#### +Usage:  
#### +    python auto_review_deps.py [--dry-run] [--pr <number>] [--verbose]  
#### +"""  
#### +  
#### +import subprocess  
#### +import json  
#### +import sys  
#### +import argparse  
#### +from datetime import datetime, timezone  
#### +from pathlib import Path  
#### +  
#### +# Configuration  
#### +WORK_DIR = Path("/home/flip/cryptotrader")  
#### +KNOWN_AUTHORS = {"dependabot[bot]", "app/dependabot", "Copilot", "github-actions[bot]", "m0nk111", "m0nk1111"}  
#### +  
#### +# CI check names - maps from actual GitHub check run names to mergify.yml expectations  
#### +CI_CHECK_MAPPING = {  
#### +    "Analyze (python)": "Backend (ruff + pytest)",  
#### +    "Analyze (javascript)": "Pre-commit checks",  
#### +    "Gitleaks": "Gitleaks",  
#### +    "CodeQL": "CodeQL",  
#### +    "Summary": "Summary",  
#### +}  
#### +  
#### +# Required CI checks for auto-approval  
#### +REQUIRED_CHECKS = {"Backend (ruff + pytest)", "Pre-commit checks", "Gitleaks"}  
#### +  
#### +# Dependency paths that count as limited scope  
#### +DEP_PATHS = {  
#### +    "package.json",  
#### +    "package-lock.json",  
#### +    "requirements.txt",  
#### +    "requirements-dev.txt",  
#### +    "pyproject.toml",  
#### +    "setup.py",  
#### +    "setup.cfg",  
#### +    ".pre-commit-config.yaml",  
#### +    ".editorconfig",  
#### +    "docker-compose.yml",  
#### +    "docker-compose.dev.yml",  
#### +    "Makefile",  
#### +}  
#### +  
#### +# Frontend dependency manifests  
#### +FRONTEND_PATHS = {"frontend/package.json", "frontend/package-lock.json"}  
#### +  
#### +# Core dependency paths (major updates to these need manual review)  
#### +CORE_DEPS = {"fastapi", "sqlalchemy", "pydantic", "numpy", "pandas", "uvicorn", "gunicorn"}  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def run_cmd(cmd: str, cwd=None) -> tuple[int, str, str]:  
#### +    """Run a shell command and return (exit_code, stdout, stderr)."""  
#### +    result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, cwd=cwd or str(WORK_DIR), capture_output=True, text=True)  
#### +    return result.returncode, result.stdout, result.stderr  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def get_open_dependabot_prs() -> list[dict]:  
#### +    """Get all open dependabot PRs with details."""  
#### +    rc, out, err = run_cmd(  
#### +        "gh pr list --state open --json number,title,author,createdAt,updatedAt,state,"  
#### +        "mergeStateStatus,labels,commits,statusCheckRollup,files,isDraft,body"  
#### +    )  
#### +    if rc != 0:  
#### +        print(f"Error getting PRs: {err}", file=sys.stderr)  
#### +        return []  
#### +  
#### +    prs = json.loads(out)  
#### +    # Explicit dependabot-only filtering  
#### +    return [p for p in prs if "dependabot" in p.get("author", {}).get("login", "")]  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def get_pr_check_runs(pr_number: int) -> list[dict]:  
#### +    """Get check runs for a specific PR."""  
#### +    # Get the head commit SHA  
#### +    rc, out, _ = run_cmd(f"gh pr view {pr_number} --json headRefOid")  
#### +    if rc != 0:  
#### +        return []  
#### +    head_sha = json.loads(out).get("headRefOid", "")  
#### +    if not head_sha:  
#### +        return []  
#### +  
#### +    # Get check runs for the head commit  
#### +    rc, out, _ = run_cmd(f"gh api repos/m0nklabs/cryptotrader/commits/{head_sha}/check-runs")  
#### +    if rc != 0:  
#### +        return []  
#### +  
#### +    data = json.loads(out)  
#### +    return data.get("check_runs", [])  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def check_ci_status(check_runs: list[dict]) -> tuple[bool, dict[str, str]]:  
#### +    """Check if all required CI checks pass.  
#### +  
#### +    Returns (all_pass, check_status_dict).  
#### +    """  
#### +    # Build a map of check name -> status  
#### +    check_map = {}  
#### +    for cr in check_runs:  
#### +        name = cr.get("name", "")  
#### +        status = cr.get("status", "")  
#### +  
#### +        # Map actual check names to mergify expectations  
#### +        mapped_name = CI_CHECK_MAPPING.get(name, name)  
#### +        check_map[mapped_name] = status  
#### +  
#### +    # Check required checks  
#### +    all_pass = True  
#### +    for req in REQUIRED_CHECKS:  
#### +        status = check_map.get(req)  
#### +        if status not in ("completed", "success", "passed", "COMPLETED", "SUCCESS", "pass"):  
#### +            all_pass = False  
#### +  
#### +    return all_pass, check_map  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def is_dependabot_pr(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR is from dependabot."""  
#### +    author = pr.get("author", {}).get("login", "")  
#### +    return "dependabot" in author.lower()  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def is_known_author(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR author is known."""  
#### +    author = pr.get("author", {}).get("login", "")  
#### +    return author in KNOWN_AUTHORS or "dependabot" in author.lower()  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def is_draft(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR is a draft."""  
#### +    return pr.get("isDraft", False)  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def has_conflicts(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR has merge conflicts (DIRTY status only)."""  
#### +    return str(pr.get("mergeStateStatus") or "").upper() == "DIRTY"  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def _is_dependency_manifest_file(filename: str) -> bool:  
#### +    return any(filename == path or filename.endswith("/" + path) for path in DEP_PATHS) or filename in FRONTEND_PATHS  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def count_dep_files(pr: dict) -> int:  
#### +    """Count the number of dependency manifest files changed."""  
#### +    files = pr.get("files", [])  
#### +    count = 0  
#### +    for f in files:  
#### +        filename = f.get("filename", "") if isinstance(f, dict) else str(f)  
#### +        if _is_dependency_manifest_file(filename):  
#### +            count += 1  
#### +    return count  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def is_limited_scope(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR scope is limited to dependency manifest files."""  
#### +    files = pr.get("files", [])  
#### +    for f in files:  
#### +        filename = f.get("filename", "") if isinstance(f, dict) else str(f)  
#### +        if not _is_dependency_manifest_file(filename):  
#### +            return False  
#### +    return True  # All files are dependency-related  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def get_pr_age_days(pr: dict) -> int:  
#### +    """Get the age of the PR in days."""  
#### +    created = pr.get("createdAt", "")  
#### +    if not created:  
#### +        return 0  
#### +    created_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(created.replace("Z", "+00:00"))  
#### +    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)  
#### +    return (now - created_dt).days  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def _classification_result(  
#### +    pr: dict,  
#### +    *,  
#### +    tier: int,  
#### +    action: str,  
#### +    reason: str,  
#### +    ci_pass: bool,  
#### +    check_map: dict[str, str],  
#### +    age_days: int,  
#### +    num_files: int,  
#### +) -> dict:  
#### +    return {  
#### +        "pr": pr,  
#### +        "tier": tier,  
#### +        "action": action,  
#### +        "reason": reason,  
#### +        "ci_pass": ci_pass,  
#### +        "check_map": check_map,  
#### +        "age_days": age_days,  
#### +        "num_files": num_files,  
#### +    }  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def classify_pr(pr: dict, check_runs: list[dict]) -> dict:  
#### +    """Classify a PR for auto-review.  
#### +  
#### +    Returns a dict with classification details.  
#### +    """  
#### +    ci_pass, check_map = check_ci_status(check_runs)  
#### +    age_days, num_files = get_pr_age_days(pr), count_dep_files(pr)  
#### +    is_dep = is_dependabot_pr(pr)  
#### +  
#### +    if not ci_pass:  
#### +        return _classification_result(  
#### +            pr,  
#### +            tier=3,  
#### +            action="comment",  
#### +            reason="CI checks not all passing",  
#### +            ci_pass=ci_pass,  
#### +            check_map=check_map,  
#### +            age_days=age_days,  
#### +            num_files=num_files,  
#### +        )  
#### +    elif has_conflicts(pr):  
#### +        return _classification_result(  
#### +            pr,  
#### +            tier=3,  
#### +            action="comment",  
#### +            reason="Merge conflicts detected",  
#### +            ci_pass=ci_pass,  
#### +            check_map=check_map,  
#### +            age_days=age_days,  
#### +            num_files=num_files,  
#### +        )  
#### +    elif is_draft(pr):  
#### +        return _classification_result(  
#### +            pr,  
#### +            tier=3,  
#### +            action="comment",  
#### +            reason="PR is draft",  
#### +            ci_pass=ci_pass,  
#### +            check_map=check_map,  
#### +            age_days=age_days,  
#### +            num_files=num_files,  
#### +        )  
#### +    elif (  
#### +        is_dep  
#### +        and ci_pass  
#### +        and not has_conflicts(pr)  
#### +        and not is_draft(pr)  
#### +        and age_days < 7  
#### +        and num_files <= 2  
#### +        and is_limited_scope(pr)  
#### +    ):  
#### +        # Tier 1: Auto-Merge  
#### +        return _classification_result(  
#### +            pr,  
#### +            tier=1,  
#### +            action="merge",  
#### +            reason=f"dependabot, CI pass, no conflicts, {age_days}d old, {num_files} file(s)",  
#### +            ci_pass=ci_pass,  
#### +            check_map=check_map,  
#### +            age_days=age_days,  
#### +            num_files=num_files,  
#### +        )  
#### +    elif ci_pass and not has_conflicts(pr) and not is_draft(pr) and (age_days >= 7 or num_files >= 3):  
#### +        # Tier 2: Auto-Approve  
#### +        return _classification_result(  
#### +            pr,  
#### +            tier=2,  
#### +            action="auto-approve",  
#### +            reason=f"CI pass, no conflicts, {age_days}d old, {num_files} file(s)",  
#### +            ci_pass=ci_pass,  
#### +            check_map=check_map,  
#### +            age_days=age_days,  
#### +            num_files=num_files,  
#### +        )  
#### +    else:  
#### +        # Tier 3: Manual  
#### +        return _classification_result(  
#### +            pr,  
#### +            tier=3,  
#### +            action="comment",  
#### +            reason="Does not meet auto-approval criteria",  
#### +            ci_pass=ci_pass,  
#### +            check_map=check_map,  
#### +            age_days=age_days,  
#### +            num_files=num_files,  
#### +        )  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def apply_action(pr_class: dict, dry_run: bool = False) -> str:  
#### +    """Apply the auto-review action for a classified PR.  
#### +  
#### +    Returns a message describing what was done.  
#### +    """  
#### +    pr = pr_class["pr"]  
#### +    pr_num = pr["number"]  
#### +    action = pr_class["action"]  
#### +    tier = pr_class["tier"]  
#### +    reason = pr_class["reason"]  
#### +  
#### +    if dry_run:  
#### +        return f"[DRY RUN] PR #{pr_num}: Tier {tier} -> {action} ({reason})"  
#### +  
#### +    if action == "merge":  
#### +        # Manual gatekeeper mode: mark the PR for manual merge review.  
#### +        labels = [label["name"] for label in pr.get("labels", [])]  
#### +        if "ready-for-manual-merge" not in labels:  
#### +            run_cmd(f"gh pr edit {pr_num} --add-label ready-for-manual-merge")  
#### +        return f"PR #{pr_num}: Ready for manual merge review"  
#### +  
#### +    elif action == "auto-approve":  
#### +        # Manual gatekeeper mode: mark the PR for manual merge review.  
#### +        labels = [label["name"] for label in pr.get("labels", [])]  
#### +        if "ready-for-manual-merge" not in labels:  
#### +            run_cmd(f"gh pr edit {pr_num} --add-label ready-for-manual-merge")  
#### +        return f"PR #{pr_num}: Ready for manual merge review"  
#### +  
#### +    elif action == "comment":  
#### +        # Add a comment - explicit draft handling  
#### +        if is_draft(pr):  
#### +            comment = f"🤖 Auto-review: Tier {tier} - Draft PR detected\n\n"  
#### +            comment += "This PR is in draft status and will not be auto-approved.\n"  
#### +        else:  
#### +            comment = f"🤖 Auto-review: Tier {tier} - {reason}\n\n"  
#### +            comment += f"CI pass: {pr_class['ci_pass']}\n"  
#### +            comment += f"Age: {pr_class['age_days']} days\n"  
#### +            comment += f"Files changed: {pr_class['num_files']}\n"  
#### +  
#### +        run_cmd(f'gh pr comment {pr_num} --body "{comment}"')  
#### +        return f"PR #{pr_num}: Added comment (Tier {tier})"  
#### +  
#### +    elif action == "label":  
#### +        label = str(pr_class.get("label") or "draft").strip()  
#### +        if label:  
#### +            labels = [label_info["name"] for label_info in pr.get("labels", [])]  
#### +            if label not in labels:  
#### +                run_cmd(f"gh pr edit {pr_num} --add-label {label}")  
#### +            return f"PR #{pr_num}: Added label {label}"  
#### +  
#### +    return f"PR #{pr_num}: No action needed"  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def run_auto_review(dry_run: bool = False, pr_number: int = None, verbose: bool = False):  
#### +    """Run the auto-review logic for dependency PRs."""  
#### +    print("=" * 60)  
#### +    print("Dependency PR Auto-Review")  
#### +    print("=" * 60)  
#### +  
#### +    # Get PRs  
#### +    if pr_number:  
#### +        all_prs = get_open_dependabot_prs()  
#### +        prs = [all_prs[0]] if all_prs else []  
#### +        # Filter by number if specified  
#### +        prs = [p for p in prs if p["number"] == pr_number]  
#### +    else:  
#### +        prs = get_open_dependabot_prs()  
#### +  
#### +    print(f"\nFound {len(prs)} open dependabot PRs\n")  
#### +  
#### +    results = []  
#### +    classifications = []  
#### +    for pr in prs:  
#### +        # Get check runs  
#### +        check_runs = get_pr_check_runs(pr["number"])  
#### +  
#### +        # Classify  
#### +        pr_class = classify_pr(pr, check_runs)  
#### +        classifications.append(pr_class)  
#### +  
#### +        if verbose:  
#### +            print(f"PR #{pr['number']}: {pr['title']}")  
#### +            print(f"  Author: {pr['author']['login']}")  
#### +            print(f"  Age: {pr_class['age_days']} days, Files: {pr_class['num_files']}")  
#### +            print(f"  CI pass: {pr_class['ci_pass']}")  
#### +            print(f"  Checks: {pr_class['check_map']}")  
#### +            print(f"  Merge status: {pr['mergeStateStatus']}")  
#### +            print(f"  Tier: {pr_class['tier']}, Action: {pr_class['action']}")  
#### +            print(f"  Reason: {pr_class['reason']}")  
#### +            print()  
#### +  
#### +        # Apply action  
#### +        result = apply_action(pr_class, dry_run)  
#### +        results.append(result)  
#### +  
#### +    # Summary  
#### +    print("\n" + "=" * 60)  
#### +    print("Summary:")  
#### +    print("=" * 60)  
#### +    for r in results:  
#### +        print(f"  {r}")  
#### +  
#### +    # Print tier distribution from the classifications that were actually applied.  
#### +    tiers = {}  
#### +    for pr_class in classifications:  
#### +        tier = pr_class["tier"]  
#### +        tiers[tier] = tiers.get(tier, 0) + 1  
#### +  
#### +    print(f"\nTier distribution: {tiers}")  
#### +    print(f"  Tier 1 (Manual-ready): {tiers.get(1, 0)}")  
#### +    print(f"  Tier 2 (Auto-Approve): {tiers.get(2, 0)}")  
#### +    print(f"  Tier 3 (Manual): {tiers.get(3, 0)}")  
#### +  
#### +    return results  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def main():  
#### +    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Auto-review dependency PRs")  
#### +    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="Don't apply changes")  
#### +    parser.add_argument("--pr", type=int, help="Review specific PR number")  
#### +    parser.add_argument("--verbose", "-v", action="store_true", help="Verbose output")  
#### +    args = parser.parse_args()  
#### +  
#### +    run_auto_review(dry_run=args.dry_run, pr_number=args.pr, verbose=args.verbose)  
#### +  
#### +    # Exit with appropriate code  
#### +    if args.dry_run:  
#### +        print("\n[Dry run complete - no chang  
#### diff --git a/scripts/merge_routing.py b/scripts/merge_routing.py  
#### new file mode 100644  
#### index 00000000..a0d4a05c  
#### --- /dev/null  
#### +++ b/scripts/merge_routing.py  
#### @@ -0,0 +1,488 @@  
#### +#!/usr/bin/env python3  
#### +"""  
#### +Merge routing for dependency PRs in cryptotrader.  
#### +  
#### +Routes dependency PRs into a manual gatekeeper lane.  
#### +  
#### +Usage:  
#### +    python merge_routing.py [--dry-run] [--pr <number>] [--verbose] [--route-only]  
#### +  
#### +Tier routing:  
#### +    Tier 1 (Manual-ready): dependabot + CI pass + no conflict + <7d old + patch/req/minor → mark ready for manual merge  
#### +    Tier 2 (Manual-ready): CI pass + no conflict + (>=7d old OR major OR 3+ deps) → mark ready for manual merge  
#### +    Tier 3 (Manual):       conflicts, failing CI, draft, do-not-merge, major core deps, >14d → notify human  
#### +"""  
#### +  
#### +import subprocess  
#### +import json  
#### +import sys  
#### +import argparse  
#### +from datetime import datetime, timezone  
#### +from pathlib import Path  
#### +from enum import Enum  
#### +from typing import Optional  
#### +  
#### +# Configuration  
#### +WORK_DIR = Path("/home/flip/cryptotrader_hermes")  
#### +KNOWN_AUTHORS = {"dependabot[bot]", "app/dependabot", "Copilot", "github-actions[bot]", "m0nk111", "m0nk1111"}  
#### +  
#### +# CI check names  
#### +CI_CHECK_MAPPING = {  
#### +    "Analyze (python)": "Backend (ruff + pytest)",  
#### +    "Analyze (javascript)": "Pre-commit checks",  
#### +    "Gitleaks": "Gitleaks",  
#### +    "CodeQL": "CodeQL",  
#### +    "Summary": "Summary",  
#### +}  
#### +  
#### +REQUIRED_CHECKS = {"Backend (ruff + pytest)", "Pre-commit checks", "Gitleaks"}  
#### +  
#### +# Dependency paths  
#### +DEP_PATHS = {  
#### +    "package.json",  
#### +    "package-lock.json",  
#### +    "requirements.txt",  
#### +    "requirements-dev.txt",  
#### +    "pyproject.toml",  
#### +    "setup.py",  
#### +    "setup.cfg",  
#### +    ".pre-commit-config.yaml",  
#### +    ".editorconfig",  
#### +    "docker-compose.yml",  
#### +    "docker-compose.dev.yml",  
#### +    "Makefile",  
#### +}  
#### +  
#### +FRONTEND_PATHS = {"frontend/package.json", "frontend/package-lock.json"}  
#### +CORE_DEPS = {"fastapi", "sqlalchemy", "pydantic", "numpy", "pandas", "uvicorn", "gunicorn"}  
#### +  
#### +# Routing config  
#### +TIER1_MAX_AGE_DAYS = 7  
#### +TIER1_MAX_DEPS = 2  
#### +TIER2_HOLD_HOURS = 48  
#### +TIER3_MAX_AGE_DAYS = 14  
#### +TIER3_AUTO_MERGE_DAYS = 30  
#### +MANUAL_MERGE_LABEL = "ready-for-manual-merge"  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +class Tier(Enum):  
#### +    TIER_1 = 1  # Manual-ready fast lane  
#### +    TIER_2 = 2  # Manual-ready guarded lane  
#### +    TIER_3 = 3  # Manual  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +class Action(Enum):  
#### +    MERGE = "merge"  
#### +    AUTO_APPROVE = "auto-approve"  
#### +    COMMENT = "comment"  
#### +    ESCALATE = "escalate"  
#### +    SKIP = "skip"  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +class MergeRoute:  
#### +    """Single PR merge routing result."""  
#### +  
#### +    def __init__(self, pr_number: int, title: str, tier: Tier, action: Action, reason: str, details: dict = None):  
#### +        self.pr_number = pr_number  
#### +        self.title = title  
#### +        self.tier = tier  
#### +        self.action = action  
#### +        self.reason = reason  
#### +        self.details = details or {}  
#### +  
#### +    def __repr__(self):  
#### +        return f"MergeRoute(PR#{self.pr_number}: Tier{self.tier.value} -> {self.action.value} ({self.reason}))"  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def run_cmd(cmd: str, cwd=None) -> tuple[int, str, str]:  
#### +    """Run a shell command and return (exit_code, stdout, stderr)."""  
#### +    result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, cwd=cwd or str(WORK_DIR), capture_output=True, text=True)  
#### +    return result.returncode, result.stdout, result.stderr  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def get_open_prs() -> list[dict]:  
#### +    """Get all open dependabot PRs with details."""  
#### +    rc, out, err = run_cmd(  
#### +        "gh pr list --state open --json number,title,author,createdAt,updatedAt,state,"  
#### +        "mergeStateStatus,labels,commits,statusCheckRollup,files,isDraft,body"  
#### +    )  
#### +    if rc != 0:  
#### +        print(f"Error getting PRs: {err}", file=sys.stderr)  
#### +        return []  
#### +    prs = json.loads(out)  
#### +    # Filter to dependabot PRs only  
#### +    return [pr for pr in prs if "dependabot" in pr.get("author", {}).get("login", "").lower()]  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def get_pr_check_runs(pr_number: int) -> list[dict]:  
#### +    """Get check runs for a specific PR."""  
#### +    rc, out, _ = run_cmd(f"gh pr view {pr_number} --json headRefOid")  
#### +    if rc != 0:  
#### +        return []  
#### +    head_sha = json.loads(out).get("headRefOid", "")  
#### +    if not head_sha:  
#### +        return []  
#### +  
#### +    rc, out, _ = run_cmd(f"gh api repos/m0nklabs/cryptotrader/commits/{head_sha}/check-runs")  
#### +    if rc != 0:  
#### +        return []  
#### +  
#### +    data = json.loads(out)  
#### +    return data.get("check_runs", [])  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def check_ci_status(check_runs: list[dict]) -> tuple[bool, dict[str, str]]:  
#### +    """Check if all required CI checks pass.  
#### +  
#### +    Returns (all_pass, check_status_dict).  
#### +    """  
#### +    check_map = {}  
#### +    for cr in check_runs:  
#### +        name = cr.get("name", "")  
#### +        status = cr.get("status", "")  
#### +  
#### +        mapped_name = CI_CHECK_MAPPING.get(name, name)  
#### +        check_map[mapped_name] = status  
#### +  
#### +    all_pass = True  
#### +    for req in REQUIRED_CHECKS:  
#### +        status = check_map.get(req)  
#### +        if status not in ("completed", "success", "passed", "COMPLETED", "SUCCESS", "pass"):  
#### +            all_pass = False  
#### +  
#### +    return all_pass, check_map  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def is_dependabot_pr(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR is from dependabot."""  
#### +    author = pr.get("author", {}).get("login", "")  
#### +    return "dependabot" in author.lower()  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def is_known_author(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR author is known."""  
#### +    author = pr.get("author", {}).get("login", "")  
#### +    return author in KNOWN_AUTHORS or "dependabot" in author.lower()  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def is_draft(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR is a draft."""  
#### +    return pr.get("isDraft", False)  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def has_conflicts(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR has merge conflicts."""  
#### +    return str(pr.get("mergeStateStatus") or "").upper() == "DIRTY"  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def _is_dependency_manifest_file(filename: str) -> bool:  
#### +    return any(filename == path or filename.endswith("/" + path) for path in DEP_PATHS) or filename in FRONTEND_PATHS  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def count_dep_files(pr: dict) -> int:  
#### +    """Count the number of dependency manifest files changed."""  
#### +    files = pr.get("files", [])  
#### +    count = 0  
#### +    for f in files:  
#### +        filename = f.get("filename", "") if isinstance(f, dict) else str(f)  
#### +        if _is_dependency_manifest_file(filename):  
#### +            count += 1  
#### +    return count  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def is_limited_scope(pr: dict) -> bool:  
#### +    """Check if PR scope is limited to dependency manifest files."""  
#### +    files = pr.get("files", [])  
#### +    for f in files:  
#### +        filename = f.get("filename", "") if isinstance(f, dict) else str(f)  
#### +        if not _is_dependency_manifest_file(filename):  
#### +            return False  
#### +    return True  # All files are dependency-related  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def get_pr_age_days(pr: dict) -> int:  
#### +    """Get the age of the PR in days."""  
#### +    created = pr.get("createdAt", "")  
#### +    if not created:  
#### +        return 0  
#### +    created_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(created.replace("Z", "+00:00"))  
#### +    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)  
#### +    return (now - created_dt).days  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def classify_pr(pr: dict, check_runs: list[dict]) -> MergeRoute:  
#### +    """Classify a PR for merge routing.  
#### +  
#### +    Returns a MergeRoute with tier and action.  
#### +    """  
#### +    ci_pass, check_map = check_ci_status(check_runs)  
#### +    age_days = get_pr_age_days(pr)  
#### +    num_files = count_dep_files(pr)  
#### +    is_dep = is_dependabot_pr(pr)  
#### +    dep_conflicts = has_conflicts(pr)  
#### +  
#### +    # Tier 3: Manual - failing CI, conflicts, draft, too old  
#### +    if not ci_pass:  
#### +        return MergeRoute(  
#### +            pr["number"],  
#### +            pr["title"],  
#### +            Tier.TIER_3,  
#### +            Action.COMMENT,  
#### +            f"CI not passing: {[k for k,v in check_map.items() if v not in ('completed','success','passed','COMPLETED','SUCCESS','pass')]}",  
#### +            {"check_map": check_map, "ci_pass": ci_pass},  
#### +        )  
#### +  
#### +    if dep_conflicts:  
#### +        return MergeRoute(  
#### +            pr["number"],  
#### +            pr["title"],  
#### +            Tier.TIER_3,  
#### +            Action.COMMENT,  
#### +            "Merge conflicts detected",  
#### +            {"check_map": check_map, "ci_pass": ci_pass, "conflicts": True},  
#### +        )  
#### +  
#### +    if is_draft(pr):  
#### +        return MergeRoute(  
#### +            pr["number"],  
#### +            pr["title"],  
#### +            Tier.TIER_3,  
#### +            Action.COMMENT,  
#### +            "PR is draft",  
#### +            {"check_map": check_map, "ci_pass": ci_pass},  
#### +        )  
#### +  
#### +    # Tier 1: Auto-Merge  
#### +    if (  
#### +        is_dep  
#### +        and ci_pass  
#### +        and not dep_conflicts  
#### +        and age_days < TIER1_MAX_AGE_DAYS  
#### +        and num_files <= TIER1_MAX_DEPS  
#### +        and is_limited_scope(pr)  
#### +    ):  
#### +        return MergeRoute(  
#### +            pr["number"],  
#### +            pr["title"],  
#### +            Tier.TIER_1,  
#### +            Action.MERGE,  
#### +            f"dependabot, CI pass, no conflicts, {age_days}d old, {num_files} file(s)",  
#### +            {"check_map": check_map, "ci_pass": ci_pass, "age_days": age_days, "num_files": num_files},  
#### +        )  
#### +  
#### +    # Tier 2: Auto-Approve (but never for drafts)  
#### +    if ci_pass and not dep_conflicts and not is_draft(pr) and (age_days >= TIER1_MAX_AGE_DAYS or num_files >= 3):  
#### +        return MergeRoute(  
#### +            pr["number"],  
#### +            pr["title"],  
#### +            Tier.TIER_2,  
#### +            Action.AUTO_APPROVE,  
#### +            f"CI pass, no conflicts, {age_days}d old, {num_files} file(s)",  
#### +            {"check_map": check_map, "ci_pass": ci_pass, "age_days": age_days, "num_files": num_files},  
#### +        )  
#### +  
#### +    # Fallback Tier 3 for PRs with passing CI that still miss fast-lane criteria.  
#### +    return MergeRoute(  
#### +        pr["number"],  
#### +        pr["title"],  
#### +        Tier.TIER_3,  
#### +        Action.COMMENT,  
#### +        "Does not meet auto-approval criteria",  
#### +        {"check_map": check_map, "ci_pass": ci_pass, "age_days": age_days, "num_files": num_files},  
#### +    )  # Manual review stays required outside the auto-approve fast lane.  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +# BLOCKED can stay stale after conflict markers disappear from the PR diff.  
#### +def resolve_block_status(pr_number: int) -> tuple[bool, str]:  
#### +    """Resolve BLOCKED merge status without automatic merge actions."""  
#### +    # Check if there are actual conflict markers in the files  
#### +    rc, diff_output, _ = run_cmd(f"gh pr diff {pr_number}")  
#### +    has_conflict_markers = '"content"' in diff_output or "<<<<<<" in diff_output  
#### +    if has_conflict_markers:  
#### +        return False, f"PR #{pr_number} has real conflict markers and must be resolved by a human before manual merge."  
#### +    else:  
#### +        # No actual conflict markers, BLOCKED may be stale.  
#### +        return True, f"PR #{pr_number} BLOCKED status looks stale; review manually before merge."  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def apply_merge_route(route: MergeRoute, dry_run: bool = False) -> str:  
#### +    """Apply the merge routing action for a classified PR.  
#### +  
#### +    Returns a message describing what was done.  
#### +    """  
#### +    pr_num = route.pr_number  
#### +  
#### +    if dry_run:  
#### +        return f"[DRY RUN] PR #{pr_num}: Tier {route.tier.value} -> {route.action.value} ({route.reason})"  
#### +  
#### +    if route.action == Action.MERGE:  
#### +        # Check if BLOCKED and resolve if needed  
#### +        if route.details.get("conflicts"):  
#### +            resolved, msg = resolve_block_status(pr_num)  
#### +            if not resolved:  
#### +                return msg  
#### +  
#### +        labels = [label["name"] for label in pr_get_labels(pr_num)]  
#### +        if MANUAL_MERGE_LABEL not in labels:  
#### +            run_cmd(f"gh pr edit {pr_num} --add-label {MANUAL_MERGE_LABEL}")  
#### +        return f"PR #{pr_num}: Ready for manual merge review - {route.reason}"  
#### +  
#### +    elif route.action == Action.AUTO_APPROVE:  
#### +        # Never auto-approve drafts - they should be COMMENT action  
#### +        labels = [label["name"] for label in pr_get_labels(pr_num)]  
#### +        if MANUAL_MERGE_LABEL not in labels:  
#### +            run_cmd(f"gh pr edit {pr_num} --add-label {MANUAL_MERGE_LABEL}")  
#### +        return f"PR #{pr_num}: Ready for manual merge review - {route.reason}"  
#### +  
#### +    elif route.action == Action.COMMENT:  
#### +        comment = f"\U0001F916 Merge routing: Tier {route.tier.value} - {route.reason}\n\n"  
#### +        comment += f"CI pass: {route.details.get('ci_pass', 'N/A')}\n"  
#### +        comment += f"Age: {route.details.get('age_days', 'N/A')} days\n"  
#### +        comment += f"Files changed: {route.details.get('num_files', 'N/A')}\n"  
#### +  
#### +        run_cmd(f'gh pr comment {pr_num} --body "{comment}"')  
#### +        return f"PR #{pr_num}: Added comment (Tier {route.tier.value})"  
#### +  
#### +    elif route.action == Action.ESCALATE:  
#### +        run_cmd(f'gh pr edit {pr_num} --add-label "do-not-merge"')  
#### +        return f"PR #{pr_num}: Escalated to manual review"  
#### +  
#### +    return f"PR #{pr_num}: No action needed"  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def pr_get_labels(pr_number: int) -> list[dict]:  
#### +    """Get labels for a PR."""  
#### +    rc, out, _ = run_cmd(f"gh pr view {pr_number} --json labels")  
#### +    if rc == 0:  
#### +        return json.loads(out).get("labels", [])  
#### +    return []  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def pr_get_updated_time(pr_number: int) -> Optional[datetime]:  
#### +    """Get the updated time of a PR."""  
#### +    rc, out, _ = run_cmd(f"gh pr view {pr_number} --json updatedAt")  
#### +    if rc == 0:  
#### +        updated = json.loads(out).get("updatedAt", "")  
#### +        if updated:  
#### +            return datetime.fromisoformat(updated.replace("Z", "+00:00"))  
#### +    return None  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def route_prs(  
#### +    prs: list[dict], dry_run: bool = False, verbose: bool = False, pr_number: Optional[int] = None  
#### +) -> list[MergeRoute]:  
#### +    """Route all (or specified) PRs through merge routing.  
#### +  
#### +    Returns list of MergeRoute results.  
#### +    """  
#### +    if pr_number:  
#### +        prs = [p for p in prs if p["number"] == pr_number]  
#### +  
#### +    routes = []  
#### +    for pr in prs:  
#### +        check_runs = get_pr_check_runs(pr["number"])  
#### +        route = classify_pr(pr, check_runs)  
#### +        routes.append(route)  
#### +  
#### +        if verbose:  
#### +            print(f"PR #{pr['number']}: {pr['title']}")  
#### +            print(  
#### +                f"  Author: {pr['author']['login']}, Age: {route.details.get('age_days', '?')}d, Files: {route.details.get('num_files', '?')}"  
#### +            )  
#### +            print(f"  CI pass: {route.details.get('ci_pass', '?')}")  
#### +            print(f"  Route: Tier {route.tier.value} -> {route.action.value} ({route.reason})")  
#### +            print()  
#### +  
#### +    return routes  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +def execute_routes(rout

#### +#!/usr/bin/env python3  
#### +"""  
#### +Regression tests for merge routing and auto review dependency handling.  
#### +  
#### +Tests dependency file counting, limited-scope detection, and draft handling.  
#### +"""  
#### +  
#### +import pytest  
#### +from unittest.mock import patch  
#### +import sys  
#### +from pathlib import Path  
#### +  
#### +# Add scripts to path for imports  
#### +sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent / "scripts"))  
#### +  
#### +from merge_routing import count_dep_files, is_limited_scope, classify_pr  
#### +from auto_review_deps import (  
#### +    count_dep_files as auto_count_dep_files,  
#### +    is_limited_scope as auto_is_limited_scope,  
#### +    classify_pr as auto_classify_pr,  
#### +)  
#### +  
#### +  
#### +class TestDependencyFileHandling:  
#### +    """Test dependency file counting and scope detection."""  
#### +  
#### +    def test_count_dep_files_dependency_only(self):  
#### +        """Test that count_dep_files only counts actual dependency files."""  
#### +        pr = {  
#### +            "files": [  
#### +                {"filename": "requirements.txt"},  
#### +                {"filename": "package.json"},  
#### +                {"filename": "frontend/package-lock.json"},  
#### +                {"filename": "src/main.py"},  # Non-dependency file  
#### +            ]  
#### +        }  
#### +  
#### +        # Should count 3 dependency files, ignore src/main.py  
#### +        assert count_dep_files(pr) == 3  
#### +        assert auto_count_dep_files(pr) == 3  
#### +  
#### +    def test_frontend_source_files_are_not_dependency_manifests(self):  
#### +        """Frontend source files must not be treated as dependency-only scope."""  
#### +        pr = {  
#### +            "files": [  
#### +                {"filename": "frontend/src/App.tsx"},  
#### +                {"filename": "frontend/package.json"},  
#### +                {"filename": "requirements.txt"},  
#### +            ]  
#### +        }  
#### +  
#### +        assert count_dep_files(pr) == 2  
#### +        assert auto_count_dep_files(pr) == 2  
#### +        assert is_limited_scope(pr) is False  
#### +        assert auto_is_limited_scope(pr) is False  
#### +  
#### +    def test_is_limited_scope_false_for_non_dependency(self):  
#### +        """Test that is_limited_scope returns False for any non-dependency path."""  
#### +        pr = {  
#### +            "files": [  
#### +                {"filename": "requirements.txt"},  
#### +                {"filename": "src/business_logic.py"},  # Non-dependency path  
#### +            ]  
#### +        }  
#### +  
#### +        # Should be False because of src/business_logic.py  
#### +        assert is_limited_scope(pr) is False  
#### +        assert auto_is_limited_scope(pr) is False  
#### +  
#### +    def test_is_limited_scope_true_for_dependency_only(self):  
#### +        """Test that is_limited_scope returns True when all files are dependency-related."""

#### +            "files": [  
#### +                {"filename": "requirements.txt"},  
#### +                {"filename": "package.json"},  
#### +                {"filename": "frontend/package-lock.json"},  
#### +                {"filename": ".pre-commit-config.yaml"},  
#### +            ]  
#### +        }  
#### +  
#### +        # Should be True - all files are dependency-related  
#### +        assert is_limited_scope(pr) is True  
#### +        assert auto_is_limited_scope(pr) is True  
#### +

<thinking-content-7bbeb8e1441453ad999a0bbba8a46d4b>

We need to review the PR #328. The diff shows: modifications to mergify.yml (changing auto-merge rules to manual gatekeeper), new files auto_review_deps.py and merge_routing.py, and test file test_merge_routing_regression.py.

We are asked to detect concrete high-confidence bugs before merge. We must produce findings if any.

We must analyze the code for correctness issues.

Let's examine the new scripts for possible serious bugs.

First, auto_review_deps.py:

- In `_is_dependency_manifest_file`: uses `filename == path` or `filename.endswith("/" + path)` for each path in DEP_PATHS. But DEP_PATHS contains e.g., "package.json". That works for exact match "package.json". But `filename.endswith("/" + path)` matches if filename ends with "/package.json". This would match "some/package.json". Good.

- FRONTEND_PATHS is a set with "frontend/package.json" and "frontend/package-lock.json". In _is_dependency_manifest_file returns True if filename in FRONTEND_PATHS (exact match). So "frontend/package.json" matches. That's fine.

- However, in FRONTEND_PATHS definition in auto_review_deps.py (line 56): `FRONTEND_PATHS = {"frontend/package.json", "frontend/package-lock.json"}`. But later in `count_dep_files`, they also use `_is_dependency_manifest_file`. That's consistent.

- In `merge_routing.py`, same variable definitions, but note: the diff for merge_routing.py includes a comment at the top that `FRONTEND_PATHS = {"frontend/package.json", "frontend/package-lock.json"}` (line 81). That's fine.

Potential bug: In `merge_routing.py`'s `classify_pr` function, there is fallthrough logic: it checks `if not ci_pass` return Tier3, then if dep_conflicts, then if is_draft. Then for Tier1, then for Tier2, else Tier3 fallback. This seems fine.

But there is a potential logical issue: The Tier1 condition requires `is_dep`, ci_pass, not dep_conflicts, age_days < TIER1_MAX_AGE_DAYS and num_files <= TIER1_MAX_DEPS and is_limited_scope(pr). This is okay.

But note: In `classify_pr`, after Tier1 conditions, there is a Tier2 block: `if ci_pass and not dep_conflicts and not is_draft(pr) and (age_days >= TIER1_MAX_AGE_DAYS or num_files >= 3):` This is okay.

However, after Tier2, there is a fallback Tier3 with COMMENT action. But what about a PR that is non-dependabot, with ci_pass, no conflicts, not draft, and age_days <7 and num_files <=2 but not a dependabot (so not Tier1)? Then neither Tier1 nor Tier2 conditions match (Tier2 condition: requires age_days >=7 or num_files>=3). A non-dependabot PR that is small and recent would slip to Tier3 (COMMENT). That might be intentional. But maybe the intention is that such PRs also get Tier2 (auto-approve). However the comment says Tier2 is for CI pass no conflicts + (>=7d old OR major OR 3+ deps). The code only checks age>=7 or files>=3. That matches the description. So manual PRs that are small and recent will stay at Tier3. That's intentional.

Potential issues: In `apply_merge_route` for Action.MERGE, they check `if route.details.get("conflicts")` (line with comment about BLOCKED). This is used only when action is MERGE. The `conflicts` key is added only in the Tier3 conflict branch. But in the Tier1 block, route.details does not include "conflicts". So the condition is false. That's fine.

Potential bug: In `apply_merge_route`, for ACTION.COMMENT, they construct comment string. But they are using escape: `run_cmd(f'gh pr comment {pr_num} --body "{comment}"')`. This could be problematic if the comment contains double quotes, newlines, etc. The comment string includes newlines generated by `\n`. In Python, the f-string with newline results in actual newline. When passed as shell argument, the newline will be passed as literal newline, which may be broken depending on shell. The standard gh CLI accepts `--body` with multiline strings if they are correctly passed. However, using shell=True and f-string with quotes may cause issues. But this is a potential risk. Could break comment creation. Might be a medium severity bug. But it's not a typical off-by-one or data leakage but could cause commands to fail silently. The `run_cmd` function runs via subprocess with shell=True. When a multi-line string is passed, the shell may interpret it incorrectly (e.g., the newline ends the command after the first line). Actually, subprocess with shell=True runs `/bin/sh -c "...cmd..."`. The cmd string is: `gh pr comment ... --body "..."`. Multiline content inside double quotes is allowed by shell (the newline is part of the quoted string). It should work. But there may be issues with embedded double quotes or special characters. The review might be okay.

But there is a more serious bug: In `run_cmd` in both scripts, they use `shell=True` and pass a command string without escaping arguments. This is a command injection vulnerability if any argument (like `comment` or `label`) could affect the shell. However, `comment` is constructed from fixed strings and PR number which is integer. Label is hardcoded. So no injection risk. Still, consider when comment includes user data: The comment includes PR title? No, only reason. Reason sometimes includes `CI not passing: [list of failed checks]` which includes check names. Check names could contain special characters? Typically, check names like "Backend (ruff + pytest)" include parentheses and spaces. However, that is inside the double-quoted shell argument, which is safe as long as we don't embed a quote. Could check names include backticks? Probably not. But it's a potential risk. Probably not high enough to block. Could be a lower priority.

Better to look for data integrity issues.

Let's examine `get_pr_check_runs` in both scripts: they use `gh pr view {pr_number} --json headRefOid`. If the PR doesn't exist, rc !=0 returns empty list. That's fine.

One potential issue: In `check_ci_status`, they create mapping from actual check name to mergify expectations. The `CI_CHECK_MAPPING` maps "Analyze (python)" to "Backend (ruff + pytest)", but the status value might be "completed", "success", etc. The code checks if `status not in ("completed","success","passed","COMPLETED","SUCCESS","pass")`. However, the actual check run `conclusion` field should be used, not `status`. The code uses `status` field which is "queued", "in_progress", "completed". It does not examine `conclusion`. For completed runs, `status` is "completed", but `conclusion` may be "success" or "failure". The code checks `status` not `conclusion`. This is a major bug! Let's verify: In `check_ci_status`, they do:

```
for cr in check_runs:
    name = cr.get("name", "")
    status = cr.get("status", "")
    ...
```

The GitHub check runs API returns objects with `status` (queued, in_progress, completed) and `conclusion` (success, failure, neutral, cancelled, ...). The code incorrectly uses `status` to determine if the check passed. For a completed check that succeeded, `status` is "completed". The code will see "completed" as one of the valid statuses (because they included "completed" in the set). But a completed check that failed still has `status` = "completed". The code would treat it as pass! That's a critical bug: It will falsely consider failing CI as passing. This could lead to auto-labeling PRs as ready-for-manual-merge even though CI actually failed. The script would incorrectly add the label. Since the PR is labeled ready-for-manual-merge, a human might merge without noticing CI failure if only relying on this automation.

The condition should check both status == "completed" and conclusion in ("success", "neutral"? etc.) and ideally use "conclusion". The code uses "status" incorrectly.

Let's verify the mergify.yml condition: it uses `check-success=...`. That's correct because Mergify uses the conclusion field. But the Python script uses raw status.

Thus the bug can cause false positives. That is a high-severity issue. We should report it.

Also, note that in `check_ci_status` they check that `status in ("completed","success","passed","COMPLETED","SUCCESS","pass")`. But "success" and "passed" are not valid status values; those are conclusion values. So the code may treat a check that is in "progress" if status is "success"? Unlikely, but the set is weird.

So the fix: Use `conclusion` field instead.

Mission: detect concrete, high-confidence bugs and behavior regressions before merge.
Treat this as production code review, not dependency metadata linting or style feedback.

Review standards:
1) Focus on correctness, data leakage, boundary conditions, off-by-one errors, and broken assumptions.
2) Report only findings that are at least medium severity and have a concrete failure mechanism.
3) Every finding must be GitHub-aware: include exact repository path and a 1-based line in the PR diff.
4) Tie each finding to user-visible impact, correctness risk, or metric distortion.
5) Flag missing tests when a risky code path changed and current tests would not catch it.
6) Ignore pure style and naming nits unless they hide a real defect.
7) For dependency or lockfile-only PRs, do not flag normal npm metadata churn such as dev/devOptional/optional flags unless you can show it breaks install, build, runtime loading, or security.
8) If local validation passed and the finding is only 'investigate/regenerate lockfile', return no finding.

Evidence rules:
- Do not speculate. If uncertain, skip the finding.
- Do not report advisory observations as findings. A finding must justify blocking the PR.
- Use language like: 'can cause', 'will duplicate', 'can leak', with concrete mechanism.
- Prefer one strong finding over many weak ones.

Output rules:
- Return strict JSON only.
- Schema: {"summary":"...","findings":[{"path":"...","line":123,"issue":"...","suggestion":"...","replacement":"optional code"}]}
- Findings must be actionable and patch-oriented.
- Leave replacement empty unless you can provide an exact GitHub suggestion block for that line.
- Max 5 findings.
Return ONLY JSON in this schema: {"summary":"...","findings":[{"path":"...","line":123,"issue":"...","suggestion":"...","replacement":"optional code"}]}. Limit findings to high-signal issues only (max 5).

diff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md
index ef397ba1..1cbdd3d3 100644
--- a/CHANGELOG.md
+++ b/CHANGELOG.md
@@ -1,5 +1,10 @@
 # Changelog
 
+## 2026-06-01
+### Dashboard DB Access Repair
+- Load the repo-local runtime environment file as a safe fallback when the API resolves `DATABASE_URL`, keeping explicit process environment values authoritative.
+- Add regression coverage for DB URL resolution so manually started API processes no longer break dashboard candle and market-watch endpoints.
+
 ## 2026-05-31
 ### Walk-Forward Validation Fix
 - Fix a post-merge regression in `core/strategy_eval/walk_forward.py` where warmup equity used `Decimal + float`, causing `TypeError` and breaking strict OOS walk-forward tests.
diff --git a/api/main.py b/api/main.py
index d81a86d0..60e7de5a 100644
--- a/api/main.py
+++ b/api/main.py
@@ -26,8 +26,10 @@
 from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
 from datetime import datetime, timezone
 from decimal import Decimal
+from pathlib import Path as FilePath
 from typing import Any, Literal, Optional
 
+from dotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv
 from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query, Path
 from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse, StreamingResponse
 from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
@@ -69,6 +71,42 @@
 
 logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 
+_REPO_ROOT = FilePath(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+
+
+def _runtime_env_path() -> FilePath:
+    """Resolve the repo-local runtime environment file path."""
+    env_path = os.environ.get("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE")
+    return FilePath(env_path).expanduser() if env_path else _REPO_ROOT / ".env"
+
+
+def _load_runtime_env() -> dict[str, str]:
+    """Load repo-local runtime environment defaults and return parsed values."""
+    dotenv_path = _runtime_env_path()
+    if dotenv_path.is_file():
+        load_dotenv(dotenv_path=str(dotenv_path), override=False)
+        parsed_values = dotenv_values(dotenv_path)
+        return {key: value for key, value in parsed_values.items() if isinstance(value, str)}
+    return {}
+
+
+def _get_database_url() -> str:
+    """Resolve DATABASE_URL from environment or the repo-local .env file."""
+    database_url = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")
+    if database_url and database_url.strip():
+        return database_url
+
+    runtime_env = _load_runtime_env()
+    runtime_database_url = runtime_env.get("DATABASE_URL")
+    if runtime_database_url and runtime_database_url.strip():
+        return runtime_database_url
+
+    runtime_env_path = _runtime_env_path()
+    raise RuntimeError(
+        "DATABASE_URL environment variable is required "
+        f"(checked environment and runtime env file: {runtime_env_path})"
+    )
+
 
 @asynccontextmanager
 async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
@@ -255,10 +293,7 @@ def _get_stores() -> PostgresStores:

     if _stores is None:
-        database_url = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")
-        if not database_url:
-            raise RuntimeError("DATABASE_URL environment variable is required")
-        _stores = PostgresStores(config=PostgresConfig(database_url=database_url))
+        _stores = PostgresStores(config=PostgresConfig(database_url=_get_database_url()))

+def test_database_url_loads_from_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify DATABASE_URL falls back to the configured runtime env file."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://local:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.delenv("DATABASE_URL", raising=False)
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://local:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
+def test_database_url_env_var_wins_over_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify an explicit DATABASE_URL is never overwritten by .env loading."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"

+    monkeypatch.setenv("DATABASE_URL", "postgresql://explicit:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://explicit:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
+# Regression: empty DATABASE_URL must still allow runtime env fallback loading.
+def test_database_url_empty_falls_back_to_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify DATABASE_URL present but empty falls back to runtime env file."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://fallback:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("DATABASE_URL", "")  # Empty string
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://fallback:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
+def test_database_url_whitespace_only_falls_back_to_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify DATABASE_URL with only whitespace falls back to runtime env file."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://whitespace:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("DATABASE_URL", "   ")  # Whitespace only
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://whitespace:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+

#### You are Tier1 Reviewer for a GitHub pull request.  
#### Mission: detect concrete, high-confidence bugs and behavior regressions before merge.  
#### Treat this as production code review, not dependency metadata linting or style feedback.  
####   
#### Review standards:  
#### 1) Focus on correctness, data leakage, boundary conditions, off-by-one errors, and broken assumptions.  
#### 2) Report only findings that are at least medium severity and have a concrete failure mechanism.  
#### 3) Every finding must be GitHub-aware: include exact repository path and a 1-based line in the PR diff.  
#### 4) Tie each finding to user-visible impact, correctness risk, or metric distortion.  
#### 5) Flag missing tests when a risky code path changed and current tests would not catch it.  
#### 6) Ignore pure style and naming nits unless they hide a real defect.  
#### 7) For dependency or lockfile-only PRs, do not flag normal npm metadata churn such as dev/devOptional/optional flags unless you can show it breaks install, build, runtime loading, or security.  
#### 8) If local validation passed and the finding is only 'investigate/regenerate lockfile', return no finding.  
####   
#### Evidence rules:  
#### - Do not speculate. If uncertain, skip the finding.  
#### - Do not report advisory observations as findings. A finding must justify blocking the PR.  
#### - Use language like: 'can cause', 'will duplicate', 'can leak', with concrete mechanism.  
#### - Prefer one strong finding over many weak ones.  
####   
#### Output rules:  
#### - Return strict JSON only.  
#### - Schema: {"summary":"...","findings":[{"path":"...","line":123,"issue":"...","suggestion":"...","replacement":"optional code"}]}  
#### - Findings must be actionable and patch-oriented.  
#### - Leave replacement empty unless you can provide an exact GitHub suggestion block for that line.  
#### - Max 5 findings.

We need to review the PR diff for bugs/regressions. The PR adds a new function `_get_database_url()` that looks up DATABASE_URL in environment, then falls back to a runtime env file loaded via `dotenv_values`. However, the existing code in `_get_stores()` previously just did `os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")` and if missing raised RuntimeError. The new code calls `_get_database_url()` which will load the .env file and also call `load_dotenv(... override=False)` to load into os.environ. That is fine.

But we need to examine the diff for concrete bugs. Let's inspect the diff thoroughly.

and later:

```

```

However, `_ENV_PATH` still exists and is used in old `_get_stores()`? Actually in the new code, `_get_stores()` no longer uses `_ENV_PATH` because it calls `_get_database_url()` instead. But the old code at line ~293 (in original) used `load_dotenv(_ENV_PATH, override=False)` inside `_get_stores`. The new code does not call that anymore; it relies on `_load_runtime_env()` which calls `load_dotenv(dotenv_path=str(dotenv_path), override=False)`. So the old `_ENV_PATH` and its usage are effectively replaced. However, the old code's `_get_stores` also had `if _stores is None` block: It loaded dotenv and also used the `load_dotenv` path. The new code removed that. But there is a subtlety: The old code loaded .env only if `DATABASE_URL` was missing from environment. The new code loads .env after checking environment? Actually `_get_database_url` first checks environment, and if present and non-empty, returns. If missing or empty, then loads runtime env file via `_load_runtime_env`. That function calls `load_dotenv` (which loads into os.environ) and `dotenv_values` to get parsed values. So if environment missing, it loads .env into os.environ via `load_dotenv`. But note: It calls `load_dotenv` with `override=False`, so it won't overwrite existing env vars. That's fine. However, after that, it returns the value from `runtime_env.get("DATABASE_URL")`. But note: after calling `load_dotenv`, the DATABASE_URL is also set in os.environ? Yes, because `load_dotenv` sets os.environ. But `override=False` means it won't overwrite if already set. Since we only call load_dotenv when `os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")` is falsy (empty or missing), it should be safe. However, there is a potential bug: `_load_runtime_env` calls `dotenv_values` separately to get the parsed values. But `load_dotenv` already loaded them into os.environ. But `dotenv_values` returns the parsed values dict. That's fine. However, `_load_runtime_env` returns the dict from `dotenv_values`. Then `_get_database_url` uses that dict. But note: `dotenv_values` does NOT load into os.environ; it only parses file. So it's correct.

But there is an important subtlety: The original `_get_stores` also loaded .env via `load_dotenv(_ENV_PATH, override=False)`. That was called only when DATABASE_URL was missing from environment. The new code does the same but with a potentially different path (via `_runtime_env_path`). However, in the original code, the path was `_PROJECT_ROOT / ".env"`. The new code also uses `_REPO_ROOT / ".env"`, which is the same as `_PROJECT_ROOT`. So no change. So functionally identical for default case.

But one major bug: The `_get_database_url` function does NOT set the DATABASE_URL in os.environ when it's missing but loaded from file. However, the original code used `load_dotenv` which set it into os.environ, so later calls to `os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")` would return it. The new code does not guarantee that after `_get_database_url` returns, `os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")` is set. It only sets it via `load_dotenv`. But note: `_load_runtime_env` does call `load_dotenv`. So it is set. So okay.

But there is a potential issue with `_get_database_url` function: It calls `os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")` and if present and non-empty returns. Then if not, loads .env. However, if DATABASE_URL is set in environment but with a value that is only whitespace (like "   "), the code treats it as not set and goes to .env fallback. The tests cover that. That's fine.

Now we need to see if there are any concrete bugs in the PR diff. Let's check for any missing import or function name collisions. The new code imports `Path as FilePath` and also uses `dotenv_values, load_dotenv`. The file already had `from dotenv import load_dotenv`. That's inside a try-except. This is still present earlier in the file (before the diff chunk). The new import `from dotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv` occurs later in the diff at line ~31. That's fine; Python allows multiple imports from same module. But there is a subtlety: The previous try-except might have set `load_dotenv = None` if import failed. But the new import will re-import and possibly fail? If `dotenv` is not installed, the new import will raise ImportError. However, the old code handled the missing dotenv gracefully by setting `load_dotenv = None`. The new code does not wrap in try-except. So if `dotenv` is not installed, the import of `dotenv_values` and `load_dotenv` will raise ImportError, crashing the module on startup. That is a regression: Before, the module could be used without dotenv (maybe with fallback logic); now it will fail if dotenv is missing. This is a high-confidence bug.

The PR adds: `+from dotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv`. But the existing code already has a try-except block for `from dotenv import load_dotenv`. The new import is separate and not protected. So if `dotenv` not installed, API startup fails. This is a concrete bug.

Additionally, the new code uses `dotenv_values` but does not wrap the call in a try-except. However, if dotenv is installed, fine. The risk is when dotenv is absent.

Thus we should report this as a finding.

Also note: The existing function `_get_stores()` had a try-except for `load_dotenv` import inside its body? Actually original code had inside `_get_stores`:

But if dotenv not installed, the API crashes rather than safely falling back to missing. That's a regression.

Thus we have a finding: Unconditional import of dotenv values creates import error if package missing, breaking startup. Should wrap in try-except as before.

index ef397ba1..1cbdd3d3 100644
--- a/CHANGELOG.md
+++ b/CHANGELOG.md
@@ -1,5 +1,10 @@
 # Changelog
 
+## 2026-06-01
+### Dashboard DB Access Repair
+- Load the repo-local runtime environment file as a safe fallback when the API resolves `DATABASE_URL`, keeping explicit process environment values authoritative.
+- Add regression coverage for DB URL resolution so manually started API processes no longer break dashboard candle and market-watch endpoints.
+
 ## 2026-05-31
 ### Walk-Forward Validation Fix
 - Fix a post-merge regression in `core/strategy_eval/walk_forward.py` where warmup equity used `Decimal + float`, causing `TypeError` and breaking strict OOS walk-forward tests.
diff --git a/api/main.py b/api/main.py
index d81a86d0..d7952bad 100644
--- a/api/main.py
+++ b/api/main.py
@@ -26,8 +26,14 @@
 from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
 from datetime import datetime, timezone
 from decimal import Decimal
+from pathlib import Path as FilePath
 from typing import Any, Literal, Optional
 
+try:
+    from dotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv
+except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover
+    dotenv_values = None  # type: ignore
+    load_dotenv = None  # type: ignore
 from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query, Path
 from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse, StreamingResponse
 from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
@@ -69,6 +75,44 @@
 
 logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 
+_REPO_ROOT = FilePath(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+
+
+def _runtime_env_path() -> FilePath:
+    """Resolve the repo-local runtime environment file path."""
+    env_path = os.environ.get("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE")
+    return FilePath(env_path).expanduser() if env_path else _REPO_ROOT / ".env"
+
+
+def _load_runtime_env() -> dict[str, str]:
+    """Load repo-local runtime environment defaults and return parsed values."""
+    if load_dotenv is None or dotenv_values is None:  # pragma: no cover
+        return {}
+    dotenv_path = _runtime_env_path()
+    if dotenv_path.is_file():
+        load_dotenv(dotenv_path=str(dotenv_path), override=False)
+        parsed_values = dotenv_values(dotenv_path)
+        return {key: value for key, value in parsed_values.items() if isinstance(value, str)}
+    return {}
+
+
+def _get_database_url() -> str:
+    """Resolve DATABASE_URL from environment or the repo-local .env file."""
+    database_url = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")
+    if database_url and database_url.strip():
+        return database_url
+
+    runtime_env = _load_runtime_env()
+    runtime_database_url = runtime_env.get("DATABASE_URL")
+    if runtime_database_url and runtime_database_url.strip():
+        return runtime_database_url
+
+    runtime_env_path = _runtime_env_path()
+    raise RuntimeError(
+        "DATABASE_URL environment variable is required "
+        f"(checked environment and runtime env file: {runtime_env_path})"
+    )
+

@@ -255,10 +299,7 @@ def _get_stores() -> PostgresStores:
     """Get or initialize the database stores."""
     global _stores
     if _stores is None:
-        database_url = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")
-        if not database_url:
-            raise RuntimeError("DATABASE_URL environment variable is required")
-        _stores = PostgresStores(config=PostgresConfig(database_url=database_url))

+def test_database_url_loads_from_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify DATABASE_URL falls back to the configured runtime env file."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://local:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.delenv("DATABASE_URL", raising=False)
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://local:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
+def test_database_url_env_var_wins_over_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify an explicit DATABASE_URL is never overwritten by .env loading."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://file:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("DATABASE_URL", "postgresql://explicit:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://explicit:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
+# Regression: empty DATABASE_URL must still allow runtime env fallback loading.
+def test_database_url_empty_falls_back_to_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify DATABASE_URL present but empty falls back to runtime env file."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://fallback:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("DATABASE_URL", "")  # Empty string
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://fallback:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
+def test_database_url_whitespace_only_falls_back_to_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify DATABASE_URL with only whitespace falls back to runtime env file."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://whitespace:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("DATABASE_URL", "   ")  # Whitespace only
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://whitespace:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+

<thinking-content-7bbeb8e1441453ad999a0bbba8a46d4b>

We need to review the PR diff for high-confidence bugs. The PR adds loading of database URL from a runtime .env file via `_load_runtime_env()` and `_get_database_url()`. The existing code in `_get_stores()` previously used only `os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")`. Now it calls `_get_database_url()`.

Potential issues to examine:

1. In `_get_database_url()`: the condition `if database_url and database_url.strip()` will treat an empty string as falsy, but also a whitespace-only string as falsy? Actually `database_url and database_url.strip()`: if `database_url` is a string, `database_url` is truthy if non-empty. If `database_url = "   "`, then `database_url` is truthy (non-empty string), but `database_url.strip()` is empty string (`""`), which is falsy. The condition becomes `True and False` = False. So whitespace-only strings are considered not valid, and fallback proceeds. This matches the test case.

But there's a subtle bug: In `_load_runtime_env()`, it uses `dotenv_values` and `load_dotenv` with `override=False`. The `dotenv_values` returns parsed key-value pairs from the file (including possibly comments). However, the PR also still has `load_dotenv` import and usage in `_load_runtime_env()`. But note that the existing code in `_get_stores()` previously had no dotenv support. The newly added `_load_runtime_env()` is called only inside `_get_database_url()` on fallback path. That is fine.

Now, there is a possible bug: In `_load_runtime_env()`, it calls `load_dotenv(dotenv_path=..., override=False)`. This sets the environment variables (if not already set). However, after that, it calls `dotenv_values(dotenv_path)` to read the values again. This could read the same values. But crucially, `load_dotenv` with `override=False` does NOT overwrite existing environment variables. But it might affect other parts of the code that read environment variables after this call. That's intended.

Now, check the diff: they also added a new import `from pathlib import Path as FilePath`. That is fine.

A more critical issue: In `_load_runtime_env()`, they have:

```python
def _load_runtime_env() -> dict[str, str]:
    if load_dotenv is None or dotenv_values is None:  # pragma: no cover
        return {}
    dotenv_path = _runtime_env_path()
    if dotenv_path.is_file():
        load_dotenv(dotenv_path=str(dotenv_path), override=False)
        parsed_values = dotenv_values(dotenv_path)
        return {key: value for key, value in parsed_values.items() if isinstance(value, str)}
    return {}
```

But note: after import they have `from dotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv`. Later they also have a separate `try ... except...: load_dotenv = None`. There is duplication: line 101 in the diff: `load_dotenv = None  # type: ignore[assignment]` appears inside the `except` block. Actually let's examine the full diff more carefully.

In the PR diff, they insert:

```
 try:
-    from dotenv import load_dotenv
+    from dotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv
 except ImportError:
+    dotenv_values = None  # type: ignore
     load_dotenv = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
```

Now, check for any missing handling: In `_load_runtime_env()`, after calling `load_dotenv`, they still call `dotenv_values`. Actually in python-dotenv, `dotenv_values()` does not expand variables; `load_dotenv()` does expand variables by default. So there is inconsistency: `dotenv_values` returns raw values with potential `${VAR}` parts, while `load_dotenv` would have expanded them. However, in `_get_database_url()`, they use the value from `dotenv_values` dict, not from the environment variables potentially set by `load_dotenv`. This could cause a bug if the .env file contains a variable reference like `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://${DB_USER}:...` and `DB_USER` is defined in same .env or environment. Since `dotenv_values` does not interpolate, the returned value would contain `${DB_USER}` literal, leading to an invalid connection URL. In contrast, if the user had relied on `load_dotenv` to set the environment variable and then read it via `os.environ.get`, it would be interpolated. So the new fallback path can produce a malformed DATABASE_URL. This is a concrete bug.

The old behavior (in original code) never loaded .env at all, so no issue. But now if the .env contains variable interpolation, the fallback will get a literal `${...}` string.

Impact: For users whose .env file uses variable substitution (common pattern), the API startup will fail with invalid connection string. This is a regression.

      "issue": "`dotenv_values` returns literal `${...}` placeholders instead of interpolated values; also expects `str` not `Path` in some versions. The function must use `os.environ.get` after `load_dotenv`, not `dotenv_values`, to match the original safe fallback behavior.",
      "suggestion": "Replace `_load_runtime_env` and `_get_database_url` with a version that loads the .env file via `load_dotenv` and reads `DATABASE_URL` from the environment, eliminating the use of `dotenv_values`.",
      "replacement": "def _load_runtime_env() -> None:\n    \"\"\"Load repo-local runtime environment defaults into process environment.\"\"\"\n    if load_dotenv is None:  # pragma: no cover\n        return\n    dotenv_path = _runtime_env_path()\n    if dotenv_path.is_file():\n        load_dotenv(dotenv_path=str(dotenv_path), override=False)\n\n\ndef _get_database_url() -> str:\n    \"\"\"Resolve DATABASE_URL from environment or the repo-local .env file.\"\"\"\n    database_url = os.environ.get(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n    if database_url and database_url.strip():\n        return database_url\n\n    _load_runtime_env()\n    database_url = os.environ.get(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n    if database_url and database_url.strip():\n        return database_url\n\n    runtime_env_path = _runtime_env_path()\n    raise RuntimeError(\n        \"DATABASE_URL environment variable is required \"\n        f\"(checked environment and runtime env file: {runtime_env_path})\"\n    )"
    }
  ]
}

PR #326: fix(api): load database URL from runtime env file
Local checks: ["python: pytest -q tests/test_api_health.py PASS"]
Diff follows:
diff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md
index ef397ba1..1cbdd3d3 100644
--- a/CHANGELOG.md
+++ b/CHANGELOG.md
@@ -1,5 +1,10 @@
 # Changelog
 
+## 2026-06-01
+### Dashboard DB Access Repair
+- Load the repo-local runtime environment file as a safe fallback when the API resolves `DATABASE_URL`, keeping explicit process environment values authoritative.
+- Add regression coverage for DB URL resolution so manually started API processes no longer break dashboard candle and market-watch endpoints.
+
 ## 2026-05-31
 ### Walk-Forward Validation Fix
 - Fix a post-merge regression in `core/strategy_eval/walk_forward.py` where warmup equity used `Decimal + float`, causing `TypeError` and breaking strict OOS walk-forward tests.
diff --git a/api/main.py b/api/main.py
index d81a86d0..d7952bad 100644
--- a/api/main.py
+++ b/api/main.py
@@ -26,8 +26,14 @@
 from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
 from datetime import datetime, timezone
 from decimal import Decimal
+from pathlib import Path as FilePath
 from typing import Any, Literal, Optional
 
+try:
+    from dotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv
+except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover
+    dotenv_values = None  # type: ignore
+    load_dotenv = None  # type: ignore
 from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query, Path
 from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse, StreamingResponse
 from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
@@ -69,6 +75,44 @@
 
 logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 
+_REPO_ROOT = FilePath(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+
+
+def _runtime_env_path() -> FilePath:
+    """Resolve the repo-local runtime environment file path."""
+    env_path = os.environ.get("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE")
+    return FilePath(env_path).expanduser() if env_path else _REPO_ROOT / ".env"
+
+
+def _load_runtime_env() -> dict[str, str]:
+    """Load repo-local runtime environment defaults and return parsed values."""
+    if load_dotenv is None or dotenv_values is None:  # pragma: no cover
+        return {}
+    dotenv_path = _runtime_env_path()
+    if dotenv_path.is_file():
+        load_dotenv(dotenv_path=str(dotenv_path), override=False)
+        parsed_values = dotenv_values(dotenv_path)
+        return {key: value for key, value in parsed_values.items() if isinstance(value, str)}
+    return {}
+
+
+def _get_database_url() -> str:
+    """Resolve DATABASE_URL from environment or the repo-local .env file."""
+    database_url = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")
+    if database_url and database_url.strip():
+        return database_url
+
+    runtime_env = _load_runtime_env()
+    runtime_database_url = runtime_env.get("DATABASE_URL")
+    if runtime_database_url and runtime_database_url.strip():
+        return runtime_database_url
+
+    runtime_env_path = _runtime_env_path()
+    raise RuntimeError(
+        "DATABASE_URL environment variable is required "
+        f"(checked environment and runtime env file: {runtime_env_path})"
+    )
+
 
 @asynccontextmanager
 async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
@@ -255,10 +299,7 @@ def _get_stores() -> PostgresStores:
     """Get or initialize the database stores."""
     global _stores
     if _stores is None:
-        database_url = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")
-        if not database_url:
-            raise RuntimeError("DATABASE_URL environment variable is required")
-        _stores = PostgresStores(config=PostgresConfig(database_url=database_url))
+        _stores = PostgresStores(config=PostgresConfig(database_url=_get_database_url()))
     return _stores
 
 
diff --git a/tests/test_api_endpoints.py b/tests/test_api_endpoints.py
index 405f4a6f..7b786b20 100644
--- a/tests/test_api_endpoints.py
+++ b/tests/test_api_endpoints.py
@@ -17,6 +17,55 @@
     sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
 
 
+def test_database_url_loads_from_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify DATABASE_URL falls back to the configured runtime env file."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://local:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.delenv("DATABASE_URL", raising=False)
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://local:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
+def test_database_url_env_var_wins_over_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify an explicit DATABASE_URL is never overwritten by .env loading."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://file:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("DATABASE_URL", "postgresql://explicit:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://explicit:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
+# Regression: empty DATABASE_URL must still allow runtime env fallback loading.
+def test_database_url_empty_falls_back_to_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify DATABASE_URL present but empty falls back to runtime env file."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://fallback:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("DATABASE_URL", "")  # Empty string
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://fallback:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
+def test_database_url_whitespace_only_falls_back_to_runtime_env_file(tmp_path, monkeypatch) -> None:
+    """Verify DATABASE_URL with only whitespace falls back to runtime env file."""
+    from api import main
+
+    env_file = tmp_path / ".env"
+    env_file.write_text("DATABASE_URL=postgresql://whitespace:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader\n")
+    monkeypatch.setenv("DATABASE_URL", "   ")  # Whitespace only
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CRYPTOTRADER_ENV_FILE", str(env_file))
+
+    assert main._get_database_url() == "postgresql://whitespace:test@127.0.0.1:5432/cryptotrader"
+
+
